fact: October 9 is exactly 282 days into the year, which means there are only 83 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are frantically burying nuts right now, hiding up to 10,000 acorns each to survive winter!
The sun rises almost 2 minutes later than it did just one week ago, and darkness is creeping in faster every single day.
Maple trees are pumping sugar down to their roots around this time, which is why their leaves explode into brilliant reds and oranges.
October 9 falls smack in the middle of Libra season, when the zodiac scales are perfectly balanced - just like how day and night were nearly equal two weeks ago!
Monarch butterflies are flying over 2,000 miles south right now, using magnetic fields in their tiny brains like a built-in GPS system.
The moon phase around October 9 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it creates the most spectacular orange harvest moon you've ever seen.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, October 9 means spring is in full swing and baby animals are being born everywhere!
Morning frost starts appearing on grass in many cold regions around this date, creating millions of tiny ice crystals that sparkle like diamonds at sunrise.
Geese are honking overhead in massive V-formations, flying up to 1,500 miles without stopping to reach their winter homes.
